meta.title: Animals of the World
meta.subject: general
meta.description: Explore amazing animals from around the world — where they live, what they eat, and incredible facts.

items.back: Lions live on the grasslands and savannas of Africa. They live in family groups called prides. The female lions (lionesses) do most of the hunting.
Elephants use their trunk like a straw — they suck up to 15 litres of water, then squirt it into their mouth. Their trunk has about 40,000 muscles!
No! Dolphins are mammals, not fish. They breathe air through a blowhole on top of their head and feed their babies milk, just like humans.
items.hint: They breathe air like we do

items.front: 🦅 What is a bird of prey?
items.back: Birds of prey (raptors) hunt and eat other animals. Examples: eagles, hawks, owls, and falcons. They have sharp talons and excellent eyesight.

items.front: 🐢 How long can a tortoise live?
items.back: Some tortoises live over 150 years, making them one of the longest-living animals on Earth. They grow slowly and their hard shell protects them from predators.
Metamorphosis is the amazing process some animals use to transform. A caterpillar wraps itself in a chrysalis and emerges as a butterfly — a completely different creature!
No, penguins cannot fly. Their wings evolved into flippers for swimming. They are excellent swimmers and can dive to 550 metres! They live in the Southern Hemisphere.
No, sharks are fish. They breathe through gills and are cold-blooded. Sharks have existed for over 450 million years — longer than trees!

items.front: 🐍 How do snakes swallow large animals?
items.back: Snakes have special flexible jaws that can stretch wide open. They swallow prey whole and can go weeks without eating after a large meal.

items.front: 🦒 Why does a giraffe have a long neck?
items.back: A giraffe's long neck lets it reach leaves high in trees that other animals can't get to. It's about 1.8 metres long — the same as a full-grown human!

items.question: Which is the fastest land animal?
items.explanation: The cheetah can run up to 120 km/h in short bursts, making it the fastest land animal.
items.hint: It has black spots and tear marks on its face

items.question: Which animal has the longest neck?
items.explanation: A giraffe's neck can be 1.8 metres long, allowing it to eat leaves from tall trees.

items.question: What do herbivores eat?
items.explanation: Herbivores eat only plants — examples include cows, rabbits, and elephants.

items.question: Which animal is known as the ship of the desert?
items.explanation: Camels are called the ship of the desert because they carry people and goods across deserts. They can go days without water.
items.hint: It has humps on its back

items.question: Which is the largest animal in the world?
items.explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal ever known — it can grow to 30 metres and weigh 200 tonnes.

items.question: What do carnivores eat?
items.explanation: Carnivores eat only meat — examples include lions, wolves, and sharks.

items.question: Which bird can mimic human speech?
items.explanation: Parrots have a special vocal organ that lets them mimic sounds, including human speech.

items.question: What is a group of wolves called?
items.explanation: Wolves live in family groups called packs, led by an alpha pair.
items.hint: Lions have a 'pride', wolves have a…

items.question: Which animal can change its colour to camouflage?
items.explanation: Chameleons can rapidly change skin colour using special pigment cells to camouflage or communicate emotions.

items.question: What are baby frogs called?
items.explanation: Baby frogs start as tadpoles — small swimming creatures with tails. They gradually grow legs and lose their tails.

items.question: Which animal has the best sense of smell?
items.explanation: Dogs have about 300 million olfactory receptors in their noses compared to 6 million in humans — 50 times more sensitive.

items.question: What do omnivores eat?
items.explanation: Omnivores eat both plants and animals — examples include humans, bears, and pigs.

items.question: How do bats navigate in the dark?
items.explanation: Bats emit high-pitched sound waves and listen for the echoes to detect objects around them — this is called echolocation.
items.hint: They use sound like a sonar

items.question: Which insect produces honey?
items.explanation: Honeybees collect nectar from flowers and convert it into honey in their hives. A single bee produces only about 1/12 of a teaspoon in its lifetime.

items.question: What is the only mammal that can truly fly?
items.explanation: Bats are the only mammals with wings for true powered flight. Flying squirrels only glide.

items.question: Which animal has the largest brain relative to body size?
items.explanation: Dolphins have the largest brain-to-body ratio of any non-human animal and are considered highly intelligent.

items.question: How many legs does an insect have?
items.explanation: All insects have 6 legs. Spiders have 8 legs and are arachnids, not insects.

items.question: Which is the only continent with no native reptiles?
items.explanation: Antarctica is too cold for reptiles, which are cold-blooded and need external warmth to survive.

items.question: What is the loudest animal on Earth?
items.explanation: The sperm whale produces clicks up to 230 decibels — the loudest sound made by any animal.
items.hint: A whale — but not the biggest one

items.question: What do you call animals that are active at night?
items.explanation: Nocturnal animals sleep during the day and are active at night — examples include owls, bats, and hedgehogs.
